name: PRO V500 V4 Resin Anchor
description:
Proven alternative to Hilti HIT-RE 500 V4
Anchor working life of 100 years
ETA and CE approved
WRAS approved
Tested to BS6920
Seismic loading C1 and C2
Dust free drilling compatible
Next day delivery

                    
                
            
            
                
                    A cost effective proven alternative to Hilti HIT-RE 500 V4, PRO V500 V4 is an ultimate performance epoxy chemical anchor resin for high loads and rebar connections. European Technical Approval. WRAS Approval. Seismic loads C1 and C2. The strongest resin on the market in concrete class C50/60.
Ultimate performance epoxy mortar for rebar connections and heavy duty anchoring including concrete to concrete and steel to concrete connections.
Qualified according to EAD 332402-00-0601 for concrete to concrete connections.
Works in diamond cored, wet or underwater holes, extremely deep embedments and large diameters.
Base materials: Concrete (cracked), Concrete (uncracked), Natural stone

Base material condition: dry, wet, water-filled, submerged
Approvals / test reports: ETA, WRAS, Fire, Seismic

name: PRO V ASPHALT ANCHOR
description:
Secure fixing into asphalt
High loads
No expansion stress
Fast curing
Versatile
Resilient

                    
                
            
            
                
                    The PRO V asphalt anchor is a high strength, internally threaded socket combined with PROV 200 injection resin, a chemical anchor resin designed for asphalt, tarmac, concrete and solid substrates.
It is suitable for temporary or permanent fixings including cycle lane bollards, traffic calming measures, parking barriers, fencing, shelters and edge protection. Performance loads up to 35kN in recent tests.
Why expansion anchors don't work in asphaltTraditionally, expansion anchors designed for concrete (plug and screw, screw bolts etc) have been used in asphalt. Expansion anchors work by constantly pressing on the drill hole wall and are held in place by friction locking. As long as the base material does not yield, the anchor will remain in place.It is estimated that asphalt is twenty times more yielding than concrete.The constant pressure exerted by expansion anchors causes the asphalt to yield with subsequent loss of friction locking, resulting in the failure of the anchor typically within days. It therefore follows that expansion anchors cannot safely be used in asphalt. 
Why PRO V asphalt anchors work in asphalt
In contrast to expansion anchors, PRO V asphalt anchors combines PRO V200 injection resin with a high strength internally threaded socket to chemically bond to the base material imparting no stress either during the installation or the lifetime of the anchor. 
Using the PRO V asphalt anchor negates the need to dig out sections of asphalt and pour concrete to form a fixing point enabling you to fix directly into the asphalt.
How PRO V asphalt anchors work
To install PRO V asphalt anchors, a hole is drilled in the asphalt and filled with PRO V200 injection resin. The internally threaded socket is slowly twisted into the resin until it is flush with the surface. The displaced resin is forced into the cracks and voids in the asphalt and any gravel layer below, creating a very strong bond. The installed anchor has now formed the equivalent of a concrete plug or cone in the asphalt. 
Base materials: asphalt, tarmac, concreteBase material condition: dry, wet, water filled.

name: INTERNALLY THREADED SLEEVE
description:
High performance loads
Close space and edge distances
Internally threaded to allow flush installation
Available in zinc plate, A2-304 stainless steel and A4-316 stainless steel
An alternative to the Hilti HIS-N and HIS-RN internally threaded sleeves
Bespoke diameters and lengths on request

                    
                
            
            
                
                    A high performance internally threaded sleeve for use with foil adhesive capsules and injection resin in C20/25 to C50/60 concrete. Designed to provide safe solutions in structural anchoring applications.
Available in zinc plate, A2-304 stainless steel and A4-316 stainless steel. 
An alternative to the Hilti HIS-N and HIS-RN internally threaded sleeves.
Sizes: M8 - M36 as standard with bespoke diameters and lengths on request.
Installation


Inject PRO V500 V4 or PRO V200 V4 injection resin into the cleaned drill hole and insert the sleeve slowly with a slight twisting motion until flush with the surface.
Remove any excess resin and leave anchorage undisturbed until curing time has elapsed.

name: Mesh Sleeve
description:
Composite mesh sleeves for fastenings in masonry and hollow base materials.
Use with PRO V200 injection resin.
Use with chemical V anchor rods and internally threaded inserts.
The centring ring holds the anchor rod in place during resin curing.
Range of sizes available.

                    
                
            
            
                
                    Composite mesh sleeve for fastenings in masonry and hollow base materials. Approved for use with PRO V200 injection resin in combination with chemical V anchor rods in the range of M6, M8, M10 and M12. 
An alternative to the Hilti HIT SC screen tube and HIT S plastic screen tube.
Can also be used in combination with internally threaded inserts.
Installation

Place the mesh sleeve in the cleaned drill hole and fill with PRO V200 injection resin from the base of the mesh sleeve.
Simultaneously push and twist the chemical V anchor rod into the resin until it reaches the base of the mesh sleeve. The twisting action forces the resin through the sleeve and into the base material.
The centring ring holds the anchor rod in place during resin curing and prevents resin backflow out of the sleeve.

name: FIS V Injection Mortar
description:
The versatile injection mortar system for anchoring into masonry and cracked concrete.
                    
                
            
            
                
                    The FIS V is a 2-component injection mortar based on vinylester hybrid.




Resin and hardener are stored in two separate chambers and are not mixed and activated until extrusion through the static mixer.
The injection cartridges are quick and easy to use with the Fischer dispensers.
Partially used cartridges can be reused, simply by changing the static mixer.
Injection mortar for use with:



Threaded rods FIS A


Internal threaded anchor RG MI


Rebar anchor FRA


Concrete steel bars


Injection anchor sleeves FIS H


Aerated concrete centring sleeve PBZ


Remedial wall tie VBS 8


Weather facing reconstruction systems
